_**User**_

Make a plan to implement AI strategies for our game of Tarock per game type. Here's detailed descriptions of strategies:
Spodaj je praktičen “strategy spec” za AI igralce. Namenoma ga pišem kot heuristike + scoring funkcije, ne kot človeške nasvete tipa “igraj po občutku”.

Temeljim na standardnem slovenskem taroku: paket ima 54 kart, od tega 22 tarokov in 4 barve po 8 kart; pozitivne igre ciljajo vsaj 36 od 70 kartnih točk; pri normalnih igrah se lahko štejejo bonusi, kot so trula, kralji, pagat ultimo in valat; pri negativnih igrah mora igralec prehiteti najvišjo karto, če lahko, pagata pa ne sme igrati, razen ko je prisiljen. ([Pagat][1])

---

## 1. Najprej naredi skupni “hand evaluator”

Vsak AI naj si roko pretvori v feature set. To boš potem uporabljal za bidding, talon izbiro, napovedi in igranje.

```js
features = {
  tarokCount,
  highTarokCount,        // npr. XVI-XXI + Škis
  midTarokCount,         // npr. VIII-XV
  lowTarokCount,         // I-VII
  hasSkis,
  hasMond,
  hasPagat,
  trulaCount,            // Škis, Mond, Pagat
  kingsCount,
  queensCount,
  pointCardsValue,       // surova vrednost dragih kart
  suitLengths: {S,C,H,D},
  suitHighCards: {S,C,H,D}, // K,Q,C,J...
  voidSuits,             // barve brez kart
  singletonSuits,        // barve z 1 karto
  weakSuitExits,         // nizke karte, s katerimi lahko izgubljaš štih
  sureWinnersEstimate,
  unavoidableWinnerEstimate,
  pagatUltimoPotential,
  kingUltimoPotential,
  mondCaptureRisk,
  talonDependency
}
```

Za pozitivne igre je dobra roka običajno: veliko tarokov, več visokih tarokov, trula/kralji, malo “mrtvih” barvnih kart, možnost narediti renonce/void v barvi po talonu. Za negativne igre je ravno obratno: čim manj zmagovalnih kart, čim več nizkih “exit” kart, čim manj tarokov in čim manj kraljev/dam/kavalov.

Priporočam, da roki izračunaš več ločenih ocen, ne ene same:

```js
positiveStrength =
  2.0 * tarokCount
+  3.0 * highTarokCount
+  4.0 * hasSkis
+  3.0 * hasMond
+  2.0 * trulaCount
+  1.5 * kingsCount
+  1.0 * voidSuits
-  1.5 * mondCaptureRisk
-  1.0 * talonDependency;

negativeStrength =
  2.0 * weakSuitExits
-  3.0 * sureWinnersEstimate
-  2.5 * tarokCount
-  3.0 * kingsCount
-  2.0 * queensCount
-  4.0 * hasSkis
-  3.0 * hasMond;

soloStrength =
  positiveStrength
+  2.0 * tarokCount
+  2.0 * highTarokCount
+  1.5 * voidSuits
-  2.0 * relianceOnPartner;
```

Te uteži niso “pravilne”, so dober začetni baseline. Najboljši pristop bo kasneje self-play tuning: AI naj odigra tisoče partij in uteži prilagodi po EV.

---

## 2. Bidding strategija

Bidding naj ne bo “imam 7 tarokov, torej enka”. Bolje je:

```js
for each legalContract:
  ev = estimateContractEV(hand, contract, auctionState, scoreState)
choose highest EV contract if ev > passEV + riskMargin
otherwise pass
```

EV naj vključuje:

```js
EV =
  P(winGame) * expectedWinScore
- P(loseGame) * expectedLoseScore
+ P(bonusSuccess) * bonusValue
- P(bonusFail) * bonusPenalty
+ radlValueAdjustment
- riskPenalty
```

Pri normalnih pozitivnih igrah se osnovna vrednost igre poveča za razliko; pri višjih igrah od berača naprej ni razlike in ni bonusov, razen posebnosti monda pri solo brez. ([Pagat][1])

### Osnovni bidding pragovi

Te prage lahko uporabiš kot fallback, če še nimaš Monte Carlo simulacije.

| Igra                  | Minimalen občutek roke za AI                                                                                 |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **klop**              | ne bidaj; igra se, ko vsi spustijo. AI naj samo oceni, ali lahko preživi z malo točkami.                     |
| **three / tri**       | forehand only; 5–6 tarokov, nekaj kontrole, partner ti lahko pomaga, talon 3 karte popravi roko.             |
| **two / dva**         | 6+ tarokov ali močna trula/kralji; manj talona, zato manj popravljanja.                                      |
| **one / ena**         | 7+ tarokov ali zelo močne kontrole; roka mora biti že skoraj dovolj dobra.                                   |
| **solo three**        | 8+ tarokov ali zelo močna kombinacija Škis/Mond/kralji; ne računaš na partnerja.                             |
| **solo two**          | 8–9+ tarokov, visok tarok control, malo šibkih barv.                                                         |
| **solo one**          | 9+ tarokov, visoka kontrola, roka skoraj samozadostna.                                                       |
| **berač**             | zelo veliko nizkih izhodov, malo tarokov, skoraj nič prisilnih zmagovalcev.                                  |
| **solo brez**         | izredno močna pozitivna roka brez potrebe po talonu.                                                         |
| **odprti berač**      | berač, ki preživi skoraj popolno obrambo; ne samo “dobra” negativna roka.                                    |
| **barvni valat brez** | možnost pobrati vse štihe brez tega, da taroki delujejo kot aduti. Potrebuješ zaporedne zmagovalce v barvah. |
| **valat brez**        | skoraj absolutna kontrola vseh štihov, običajno veliko najvišjih tarokov in zanesljivi barvni zmagovalci.    |

---

## 3. Izbira klicanega kralja: three, two, one

Pri igrah s partnerjem deklarer kliče kralja, lastnik tega kralja pa postane skriti partner. Partnerstvo se lahko razkrije šele med igro. ([Pagat][1])

Hevristika za izbiro klicanega kralja:

```js
for each suit where !hand.hasKing(suit):
  score =
    2.0 * shortSuitBonus(suit)       // 0-2 karti v tej barvi je pogosto dobro
  + 1.5 * abilityToTrumpSuitLater
  + 1.0 * hidePartnerPotential
  - 2.0 * dangerOfCalledKingBeingCaptured
  - 1.5 * chanceKingInTalonAndForcedSolo
choose highest score
```

Praktično:

* Če imaš kratko barvo brez kralja, je pogosto dobra za klic, ker boš kasneje lahko tarokiral.
* Če imaš v barvi veliko srednjih kart, lahko nasprotniki lažje izsilijo kralja.
* Če imaš zelo močno roko, je lahko klic manj pomemben; AI naj raje razmisli o solo igri.
* Če imaš vse kralje, je self-call sicer legalen, ampak strateško slab, ker igraš sam za nižjo vrednost kot solo. ([Pagat][1])

---

## 4. Talon strategija

Pri igrah s talonom se talon razkrije v skupinah glede na pogodbo: 3+3 pri tri/solo tri, 2+2+2 pri dva/solo dva, posamezno pri ena/solo ena. Deklarer izbere skupino, doda karte v roko in nato odloži enako število kart v svoj kup; 5-točkovnih kart, torej kraljev in trule, ne sme odložiti, druge taroke pa lahko, vendar odprto. ([Pagat][1])

Za AI:

```js
for each talonOption:
  newHand = hand + talonOption
  bestDiscard = findBestLegalDiscard(newHand, contract)
  score =
    gameWinProbability(newHand - bestDiscard)
  + discardPointsBanked(bestDiscard)
  + createdVoidBonus(bestDiscard)
  + bonusPotential(newHand - bestDiscard)
  - exposedTrumpDiscardPenalty(bestDiscard)
choose max(score)
```

### Kaj odlagati v pozitivnih igrah

Najprej odlagaj karte, ki zmanjšujejo tveganje, ne nujno kart z najmanj točkami.

Dobra odlaganja:

* dame/kavali/fanti iz barv, kjer nimaš kralja in verjetno ne boš zmagoval,
* srednje/visoke barvne karte, ki bi te prisilile v drage izgubljene štihe,
* karte, s katerimi ustvariš void v barvi,
* nizke taroke samo, če imaš veliko tarokov in ti odprto odlaganje ne škodi preveč.

Slaba odlaganja:

* taroki, če imaš že tako malo adutov,
* karte, ki ščitijo tvojega monda,
* karte, ki so pomembne za pagat ultimo ali valat linijo,
* edina nizka izhodna karta v nevarni barvi.

---

# Strategije po igrah

## 5. Klop

Cilj: vzeti čim manj kartnih točk oziroma idealno nobenega štiha. Klop je vsak zase; v prvih šestih štihih se karta iz talona doda kot “vitamin” zmagovalcu štiha, zato so zgodnji štihi posebej nevarni. ([Pagat][1])

### Hand evaluation za klop

```js
klopRisk =
  4.0 * unavoidableWinnerEstimate
+  3.0 * highTarokCount
+  3.0 * kingsCount
+  2.0 * queensCount
+  1.5 * suitShortageWithTaroks
-  2.0 * lowExitCards
-  1.5 * longLowSuits
```

### Igranje klopa

AI naj igra po prioritetah:

1. Če lahko izgubi štih, naj izgubi štih.
2. Če mora prehiteti, naj prehiti z najmanjšo možno karto.
3. Če bo verjetno pobral štih, naj pobere s čim manjšo karto.
4. Izogiba naj se pobiranju prvih šestih štihov zaradi talonskih “vitaminov”.
5. Visoke karte naj poskuša odvreči v štihe, kjer je že jasno, da ne bo pobral.
6. Taroke naj hrani kot nevarnost, ne kot moč. V negativnih igrah je tarok pogosto liability.

Pseudocode:

```js
function playKlop(legalCards, trickState) {
  losingCards = cardsThatDoNotWin(legalCards, trickState)

  if (losingCards.length > 0) {
    return lowestPointHighestSafety(losingCards)
  }

  // forced to win
  return minimalWinningCard(legalCards, trickState)
}
```

Defenzivno proti igralcu, ki očitno beži proti “nula štihov”: če lahko izbiraš, ne jemlji nase nevarnosti samo zato, da njega rešiš. Klop je vsak zase.

---

## 6. Three / Tri / Trojka

Cilj: deklarer + skriti partner vzameta vsaj 36 točk. Deklarer kliče kralja in vzame 3 karte iz talona. ([Pagat][1])

### Kdaj jo igrati

Tri je “popravljiva” igra, ker dobiš največ talona. AI naj jo izbere, ko:

```js
tarokCount >= 5
AND positiveStrength >= medium
AND hasAtLeastOneControlCard
AND notTooManyUnprotectedHighSuitCards
```

### Deklarer strategija

* Kliči kralja v barvi, kjer imaš kratkost ali potencial za tarokiranje.
* Po talonu ustvari void, če lahko.
* Prvih nekaj štihov uporabljaj za identifikacijo partnerja.
* Ne razkrivaj partnerja po nepotrebnem, razen če moraš rešiti točke.
* Če imaš Škisa in partner verjetno ima Monda, pazi, da ga ne ujameš po nepotrebnem.
* Če imaš veliko tarokov, lahko vodiš taroke, da izčrpaš obrambo.
* Če imaš malo tarokov, vodi barve, kjer imaš kontrolo, in pusti partnerju, da dela.

### Partner strategija

Partner naj:

* ostane skrit, dokler ni koristno razkriti se,
* deklarerju podaja točke, ko je jasno, da bo deklarerjev štih,
* ne jemlje deklarerju štihov z nepotrebnim tarokom,
* zaščiti klicanega kralja, če je možno,
* ne napoveduje agresivnih bonusov, razen če ima dovolj informacij.

### Obramba

Obramba naj:

* poskuša odkriti partnerja,
* napade klicano barvo, če je smiselno,
* sili deklarerja v porabo tarokov,
* hrani visoke taroke za trenutke, ko lahko vzame veliko točk,
* pazi na pagat ultimo in kralj ultimo.

---

## 7. Two / Dva / Dvojka

Podobna kot tri, ampak deklarer vzame samo 2 karti. Tveganje je večje, ker ima manj možnosti popraviti roko.

### Bidding

```js
bidTwo if:
  tarokCount >= 6
  OR (tarokCount >= 5 AND hasSkis AND hasSeveralKings)
  AND talonDependency <= medium
```

### Strategija

Dvojka zahteva bolj disciplinirano igro:

* Talon izbira naj bolj ceni ustvarjanje voida kot surove točke.
* Ne igraj preveč pasivno; vrednost igre je višja, ampak roka mora sama narediti več.
* Če nimaš dovolj tarokov, ne vleci tarokov iz nasprotnikov — s tem lahko pomagaš obrambi.
* Če imaš partnerja, čim prej ugotovi, ali je partner taročno močan ali samo “kralj z nekaj točkami”.

AI naj bo pri dvojki precej bolj konservativen kot pri tri.

---

## 8. One / Ena / Enka

Deklarer vzame samo 1 karto iz talona. To je že skoraj “moja roka mora biti dovolj dobra sama”.

### Bidding

```js
bidOne if:
  tarokCount >= 7
  AND highTarokCount >= 2
  AND positiveStrength high
  AND talonDependency low
```

### Deklarer strategija

* Klic partnerja je še vedno pomemben, ampak ne sme biti tvoja glavna rešilna bilka.
* Talon karto oceni predvsem po tem, ali popravi strukturo roke.
* Če imaš kontrolo, vodi igro; če čakaš, lahko obramba najde tvoje šibke barve.
* Napoved pagat ultimo samo, če imaš dovolj tarokov, da izčrpaš obrambo.

### Obramba

Proti enki je pogosto prava strategija hitrejši pritisk:

* napadi barve, kjer deklarer verjetno nima kontrole,
* sili taroke ven,
* če vidiš partnerja, ki pomaga deklarerju, ga poskusi prehiteti v dragih štihih.

---

## 9. Solo three

Deklarer igra sam proti trem, vzame 3 karte iz talona. To je prva prava solo pozitivna igra.

### Bidding

```js
bidSoloThree if:
  soloStrength >= mediumHigh
  AND tarokCount >= 8
  AND (hasSkis OR hasMond OR highTarokCount >= 3)
```

### Deklarer strategija

* Talon uporabi za odstranjevanje nevarnih barv.
* Ustvari void, če lahko.
* Ne zapravljaj Škisa za majhne štihe.
* Če imaš Monda brez Škisa, oceni capture risk. Mond je močan, ampak proti Škisu nevaren.
* Če imaš Pagata, ga ne obravnavaj kot normalnega taroka; ali ga zaščiti za ultimo ali ga uporabi, ko ni nevarnosti.
* V solo igrah je cilj pogosto “kontrolirati tempo”: ti želiš odločati, ali se igra tarok ali barva.

### Obramba proti solo

Obramba naj igra kot ekipa:

* če ima obramba več tarokov kot deklarer, naj vleče taroke,
* če je deklarer očitno taročno zelo močan, naj raje vodi barve in ga sili v tarokiranje,
* ne meči točk v štihe, ki jih deklarer zanesljivo vzame,
* komuniciraj samo skozi legalno igro: visoka karta v obrambnem štihu pomeni “točke sem pripravljen dati”, nizka karta pomeni “ne vlagaj”.

---

## 10. Solo two

Solo two je močnejša verzija solo three: manj talona, več potrebne kontrole.

### Bidding

```js
bidSoloTwo if:
  tarokCount >= 8 or 9
  AND highTarokCount >= 3
  AND hasReliableEntryCards
  AND talonDependency lowMedium
```

### Strategija

* Talon ne sme biti “upam, da me reši”; talon naj samo optimizira.
* Deklarer naj hitro ugotovi, ali lahko igra na razliko/bonuse ali samo varno zmago.
* Če ima veliko tarokov, naj pogosto začne z vlečenjem tarokov.
* Če ima močne barvne kralje, naj jih ne igra v trenutku, ko je velika možnost, da je nekdo void in bo tarokiral.

Obramba naj pri solo two hitreje kontra-agresivno išče prvi velik štih, ker deklarer običajno nima veliko slabosti.

---

## 11. Solo one

Solo one je zelo močna izjava: vzameš samo eno karto in igraš sam.

### Bidding

```js
bidSoloOne if:
  tarokCount >= 9
  AND highTarokCount >= 3
  AND hasSkisOrVerySafeMond
  AND weakSuitCount <= 1
```

### Deklarer strategija

* Igraj kot “control deck”.
* Vodi taroke, če imaš dolžino in višino.
* Če imaš zelo močne barve, najprej poberi varne kralje, preden obramba naredi void.
* Pagat ultimo napovej samo z zelo visoko zanesljivostjo.
* Če nimaš potrebe po bonusih, igraj najvarnejšo linijo do 36+, ne najlepše linije.

### AI scoring

Pri solo one naj bo `riskPenalty` večji, ker je padec drag:

```js
EVsoloOne =
  winProbability * expectedScore
- lossProbability * contractValue
- 1.5 * uncertaintyPenalty
```

---

## 12. Berač

Cilj: ne vzeti nobenega štiha. Deklarer ne menja talona. V negativnih igrah moraš prehiteti, če lahko, in pagata ne smeš igrati, razen če si prisiljen. ([Pagat][1])

### Dobra berač roka

* veliko nizkih barvnih kart,
* malo ali nič visokih figur,
* malo tarokov,
* taroki naj bodo nizki in “varni”,
* čim manj situacij, kjer boš prisiljen prehiteti,
* dolge nizke barve so dobre,
* singleton kralj/dama je grozen.

### Bidding

```js
bidBeggar if:
  negativeStrength >= high
  AND unavoidableWinnerEstimate == 0 or veryLow
  AND highTarokCount == 0
  AND kingsCount == 0
  AND hasEnoughLowExits
```

### Igranje berača kot deklarer

* Vodi karto, ki ima največ možnosti izgubiti.
* Izogibaj se odpiranju barve, kjer imaš drugo ali tretjo najvišjo karto.
* Če imaš dolgo nizko barvo, jo uporabljaj kot varno izhodno pot.
* Ne izigraj karte, ki bo kasneje ustvarila prisilno zmago, razen če moraš.
* Vedno simuliraj “kaj ostane po tem štihu”.

Pseudocode:

```js
function playBeggarDeclarer(legalCards, state) {
  candidates = legalCards.map(card => ({
    card,
    immediateWinRisk: chanceCardWinsTrick(card, state),
    futureTrapRisk: estimateFutureForcedWin(card, state),
    exitPreservation: preservesLowExitCards(card, state)
  }))

  return minBy(candidates,
    5 * immediateWinRisk
  + 3 * futureTrapRisk
  - 2 * exitPreservation)
}
```

### Obramba proti beraču

Cilj obrambe je prisiliti berača, da vzame en štih.

* Vodi nizke karte v barvah, kjer sumiš, da ima berač višje.
* Če je berač že pokazal, da nima neke barve, vodi taroke ali barve, ki ga prisilijo v tarok.
* Ne pokrivaj po nepotrebnem, če s tem rešiš berača.
* Če si pred beračem, pripravi past: odigraj karto, ki jo mora prehiteti.
* Če si za beračem, pusti njegov zmagovalni štih pri življenju, če lahko.

---

## 13. Solo brez / Brez talona

Deklarer igra sam, brez talona, talon gre nasprotnikom. Cilj je vsaj 36 točk, brez bonusov in brez razlike; to je pogosto “imam zelo močno roko ali pa moram pobegniti iz klopa/berača”. ([Pagat][1])

### Bidding

```js
bidSoloWithout if:
  soloStrength >= veryHigh
  AND tarokCount >= 9
  AND highTarokCount >= 3
  AND expectedPointsWithoutTalon >= 36
```

### Deklarer strategija

* Ker talon pripade obrambi, moraš računati, da ima obramba dodatne točke.
* Ne igraj na lepoto; igraj na zanesljivih 36+.
* Zgodaj poberi varne točke.
* Če imaš Škisa, kontroliraj Monda.
* Če imaš Monda brez Škisa, ne dovoli lahkega ulova.
* Taroke vodi, če imaš dolžino. Če si kratek v tarokih, vodi varne barvne zmagovalce.

### Obramba

* Ker obramba dobi talon, naj igra potrpežljivo.
* Cilj je deklarerja držati pod 36, ne nujno pobrati spektakularnih štihov.
* Hrani visoke taroke za njegove visoke točkovne karte.
* Če lahko ujameš Monda s Škisom, je to individualna kazen za igralca Monda tudi pri solo brez. ([Pagat][1])

---

## 14. Odprti berač

Enako kot berač, ampak po prvem štihu deklarer razkrije karte. Zato mora AI pri odprtem beraču pred biddingom oceniti skoraj perfect-play situacijo. ([Pagat][1])

### Bidding

```js
bidOpenBeggar if:
  negativeStrength >= veryHigh
  AND minimaxForcedWinForDeclarer(hand) == false
```

Tukaj priporočam minimax solver, ne navadne hevristike.

Po prvem štihu ima obramba popolne informacije o deklarerjevi roki, zato:

```js
function canDeclarerSurviveOpenBeggar(openHand, visibleState) {
  return minimax(
    state,
    declarerGoal = "takeNoTricks",
    defendersGoal = "forceDeclarerTrick"
  )
}
```

### Deklarer strategija

* Prvi štih je kritičen, ker pred razkritjem še lahko skriješ strukturo.
* Izberi prvi lead, ki pusti največ varnih nadaljevanj.
* Po razkritju igraj minimax: ne najnižja karta avtomatsko, ampak karta, ki ne ustvari prisilne zmage čez 2–4 štihe.

### Obramba

* Po razkritju naj ne uporablja random heuristike.
* Vsak legalni lead naj simulira do konca ali vsaj z globino 4–6.
* Cilj je najti barvo, kjer ima deklarer samo višje karte od obrambe.

---

## 15. Barvni valat brez

Cilj: pobrati vse štihe, ampak taroki niso aduti, razen ko je tarok izigrana barva. Če nekdo ne more slediti barvi, mora še vedno igrati taroka, vendar tarok ne zmaga, če ni tarok izigrana barva. ([Pagat][1])

To je zelo drugačen contract. Tarok count sam po sebi ni dovolj.

### Dobra roka za barvni valat

* imaš zaporedne najvišje karte v več barvah,
* imaš dovolj visokih tarokov za trenutke, ko vodiš taroke,
* imaš možnost voditi vsako barvo tako, da nihče nima višje karte,
* nimaš “luknje” v barvi, kjer bi nasprotnik lahko imel višjo karto po tem, ko ti odigraš nižjo.

### Bidding

```js
bidColourValatWithout if:
  allTricksWinSimulation(hand, trumpMode = "colourValat") >= veryLikely
```

Tu ne uporabljaj preprostega `positiveStrength`. Uporabi simulacijo.

### Deklarer strategija

* Igraj zmagovalce od vrha navzdol.
* Najprej izprazni barve, kjer imaš celotno zgornjo sekvenco.
* Ne odpiraj tarokov, če nimaš kontrole tarok sekvence.
* Vsak lead naj vpraša: “ali lahko katerikoli nasprotnik še ima višjo karto te barve?”
* Če je odgovor da, contract verjetno ni varen.

Pseudocode za lead:

```js
function playColourValat(cardOptions, state) {
  winningLeads = cardOptions.filter(card =>
    cardIsGuaranteedWinnerInLedSuit(card, state, colourValatRules)
  )

  return leadThatMaintainsMostFutureGuaranteedWinners(winningLeads)
}
```

### Obramba

Obramba potrebuje samo en štih.

* Hrani “stoppers” — najvišje karte v barvah, kjer deklarer ni dokazal kontrole.
* Ne meči visokega stopperja prezgodaj, če ni prisiljen.
* Če lahko slediš barvi in imaš višjo karto, jo uporabi.
* Če vidiš luknjo v deklarerjevi sekvenci, čakaj na pravi trenutek.

---

## 16. Valat brez

Cilj: pobrati vse štihe v normalni tarok logiki, brez talona. To je ekstremna igra.

### Bidding

```js
bidValatWithout if:
  guaranteedOrNearGuaranteedAllTricks(hand, normalTrumpRules) == true
```

Hevristični pogoji:

```js
tarokCount >= 10 or 11
AND hasSkis
AND hasMond or manyTopTaroks
AND highTarokCount veryHigh
AND suitLoserCount == 0
AND noUnprotectedLowCards
```

Ampak za AI naj bo to skoraj vedno solver/simulacija, ne samo threshold.

### Deklarer strategija

* Vodi najvišje taroke, če imaš taročno dominanco.
* Odstrani nasprotnikove taroke, preden igraš ranljive kralje.
* Če imaš barvne zmagovalce, jih igraj šele, ko je varno pred tarokiranjem.
* Ne dovoli obrambi, da pride na lead.
* Vsako karto oceni po tem, ali ohranja 100% win chain.

### Obramba

Obramba ima samo en cilj: vzeti en štih.

* Če imaš Škisa ali visoke taroke, jih hrani za prekinitev.
* Če deklarer vodi nizko barvo, tarokiraj, če lahko.
* Ne meči edinega stopperja v štih, ki je že izgubljen.
* Če lahko prisiliš deklarerja v barvo, kjer nima najvišje karte, to naredi takoj.

---

# Bonusi kot dodatne strategije

Bonusi v normalnih igrah so ločeni od osnovne igre; možno je zmagati igro, a izgubiti bonus ali obratno, zato naj AI bonuse obravnava kot samostojne cilje z lastnim EV. ([Pagat][1])

## Trula

Cilj: pobrati Škisa, Monda in Pagata.

Napovej trulo samo, če:

```js
trulaCount >= 2
AND missingTrulaCaptureChance high
AND gameControl high
```

Igra:

* Če imaš Škisa in Monda, pazi na Pagata.
* Če imaš Monda brez Škisa, ne igraj ga v situacijo, kjer ga Škis ujame.
* Če imaš Pagata, ga ne zapravi zgodaj.

## Kralji

Cilj: pobrati vse 4 kralje.

Napovej, če:

```js
kingsCount >= 3
OR (kingsCount >= 2 AND partnerLikelyHasKing AND controlHigh)
```

Igra:

* Kralje igraj, ko je manj možnosti, da jih nekdo tarokira.
* Če si partner in imaš klicanega kralja, ga ne žrtvuj prezgodaj.
* Obramba naj poskuša kralje tarokirati, če sumi na napoved.

## Pagat ultimo

Cilj: zadnji štih dobiti s Pagatom. Če ga igraš v zadnjem štihu in ne zmaga, se bonus šteje kot izgubljen; pri napovedanem bonusu moraš Pagata hraniti, dokler pravila to dovoljujejo. ([Pagat][1])

Napovej samo, če:

```js
hasPagat
AND tarokCount high
AND canExhaustOpponentTaroks
AND hasEnoughControlToReachLastTrick
```

Igra:

* Vodi taroke, da izprazniš nasprotnike.
* Štej preostale taroke.
* Če je zunaj še en sam tarok nad Pagatom, ultimo ni varen.
* Če si obramba, hrani en tarok samo zato, da ubiješ Pagata.

## Kralj ultimo

Cilj: zadnji štih z dobljenim klicanim kraljem oziroma s štihom, v katerem je klicani kralj. ([Pagat][1])

Napovej, če:

```js
calledKingHolderKnownOrLikelyPartner
AND suitCanBeProtectedUntilEnd
AND trumpControlEnough
```

Obramba:

* Napadaj klicano barvo.
* Prisili kralja ven pred zadnjim štihom.
* Če se kralj skriva, spremljaj, kdo čudno varuje določeno barvo.

---

# Univerzalni card-play algoritem

Za vsak možen legalen play izračunaj score.

```js
function chooseCard(state, hand, contract, role) {
  legalCards = getLegalCards(hand, state)

  scored = legalCards.map(card => ({
    card,
    score:
      immediateTrickValue(card, state, contract, role)
    + futureControlValue(card, state, contract, role)
    + bonusProgressValue(card, state, contract, role)
    + informationValue(card, state, contract, role)
    - riskValue(card, state, contract, role)
  }))

  return maxBy(scored, "score").card
}
```

Za pozitivne igre:

```js
immediateTrickValue =
  if cardWins:
    pointsInTrick * pointNeedUrgency
  else:
    -pointsGivenToOpponents

futureControlValue =
  preserveHighTaroks
+ preserveEntries
+ createVoidOpportunities
- exposeMondRisk
- wasteTrumpOnLowValueTrick
```

Za negativne igre:

```js
immediateTrickValue =
  -hugePenalty if cardWins
  +smallBonus if cardSafelyLoses

futureControlValue =
  preserveLowExitCards
- createFutureForcedWin
```

Za valat:

```js
score =
  +1000 if cardKeepsAllTricksGuaranteed
  -10000 if cardAllowsAnyOpponentTrick
```

---

# Informacijski model za AI

Po vsakem štihu posodobi možne karte nasprotnikov.

```js
if player did not follow suit:
  possibleCards[player].removeAll(suitLed)

if player failed to play trump when void in suit:
  possibleCards[player].removeAll(taroks)

if player played high trump:
  inferLowerTrumpDistribution()

if calledSuitLed and king not played:
  reduceProbability(playerHasCalledKing)
```

Za klicanega kralja:

```js
partnerProbability[player] =
  probability(playerHasCalledKing | observedPlay)
```

AI partner naj igra drugače, ko je `partnerProbability[self] == 1`, ampak pred razkritjem naj včasih skriva pomoč.

---

# Najbolj praktičen način implementacije

Za prvo verzijo naredi tri AI težavnosti.

### Easy AI

* uporablja samo threshold bidding,
* igra najnižjo zmagovalno / najvišjo izgubljajočo karto,
* ne razume bonusov dobro.

### Medium AI

* uporablja feature scoring,
* izbira talon s scoringom,
* spremlja void barve,
* igra osnovno ekipno strategijo.

### Hard AI

* Monte Carlo bidding,
* rollout za talon,
* inference partnerja,
* minimax za berač/odprti berač/valat,
* EV-based bonus announcements.

Najbolj pomembno: za normalne igre bo heuristika dovolj dobra, za **odprti berač**, **barvni valat** in **valat brez** pa raje uporabi solver/minimax, ker so to igre, kjer ena sama napačna karta spremeni vse.

[1]: https://www.pagat.com/tarot/sltarok.html "Slovenian Tarok - card game rules"

          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "export function legalCards(hand, trick, contract) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83,
            "text": "  if (!trick.length) return [...hand];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "  const led = trick[0].card;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85,
            "text": "  const ledSuit = isTarok(led) ? \"tarok\" : led.suit;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "  const follow = hand.filter((card) =\u003e (ledSuit === \"tarok\" ? isTarok(card) : card.suit === ledSuit));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87,
            "text": "  if (follow.length) return negativeLegalSubset(follow, trick, contract);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "  const taroks = hand.filter(isTarok);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89,
            "text": "  if (taroks.length) return negativeLegalSubset(taroks, trick, contract);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "  return negativeLegalSubset([...hand], trick, contract);"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 91,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92
          },

          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "export class TarokGame {"

            "lineNumber": 379
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 380,
            "text": "  deal() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 381,
            "text": "    const deck = shuffle(createDeck());"

            "text": "    }"

            "text": "    this.activePlayers().forEach((player) =\u003e sortHand(player.hand));"
          },
          {

          },

          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 93,
            "text": "export function trickWinner(trick) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "  const contract = trick.contract;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95,
            "text": "  const tarokIds = new Set(trick.filter((play) =\u003e isTarok(play.card)).map((play) =\u003e play.card.id));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "  if (tarokIds.has(\"T1\") \u0026\u0026 tarokIds.has(\"T21\") \u0026\u0026 tarokIds.has(\"SKIS\")) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97,
            "text": "    if (contract \u0026\u0026 contract.mode === \"colourValat\" \u0026\u0026 !isTarok(trick[0].card)) return trick[0].playerId;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "    return trick.find((play) =\u003e play.card.id === \"T1\").playerId;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "  const taroks = trick.filter((play) =\u003e isTarok(play.card));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "  if (taroks.length \u0026\u0026 (!contract || contract.mode !== \"colourValat\" || isTarok(trick[0].card))) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "    return taroks.sort((a, b) =\u003e b.card.tarok - a.card.tarok)[0].playerId;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "  const ledSuit = trick[0].card.suit;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "  return trick"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "    .filter((play) =\u003e play.card.suit === ledSuit)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "    .sort((a, b) =\u003e b.card.suitStrength - a.card.suitStrength)[0].playerId;"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "export function wouldWin(card, trick, contract = null) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "  const candidateTrick = [...trick, { playerId: -1, card }];"
          },
          {

            "text": "}"
          }

          {
            "lineNumber": 850,
            "text": "  finishTrick() {"

          {
            "lineNumber": 851,
            "text": "    this.game.currentTrick.contract = this.game.contract;"

          {

            "text": "    const cards = this.game.currentTrick.map((play) =\u003e play.card);"

          },

            "text": "    const mondPlay = this.game.currentTrick.find((play) =\u003e play.card.id === \"T21\");"

          {

            "text": "    const skisPlay = this.game.currentTrick.find((play) =\u003e play.card.id === \"SKIS\");"

          {
            "lineNumber": 861,
            "text": "    if (mondPlay \u0026\u0026 skisPlay \u0026\u0026 this.game.contract.mode === \"positive\") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 862,
            "text": "      this.game.capturedMondPenalty.push(mondPlay.playerId);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 863,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 864
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 865,
            "text": "    this.log(\"winTrick\", { playerId: winnerId, trick: this.game.trickNumber + 1 });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 866,
            "text": "    this.game.animation = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 867,
            "text": "      type: \"collect\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 868,
            "text": "      id: &#96;collect-${this.game.handNumber}-${this.game.trickNumber}-${winnerId}-${Date.now()}&#96;,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 869,
            "text": "      playerId: winnerId,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 870,
            "text": "      cards"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 871,
            "text": "    };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 872,
            "text": "    this.game.trickNumber += 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 873,
            "text": "    this.game.leader = winnerId;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 874,
            "text": "    this.game.activePlayer = winnerId;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 875,
            "text": "    this.game.lastTrick = {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 876,
            "text": "      winnerId,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 877,
            "text": "      plays: this.game.currentTrick.map((play) =\u003e ({ ...play }))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 878,
            "text": "    };"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 879,
            "text": "    this.game.currentTrick = [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 880
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 881,
            "text": "    if (this.activePlayers().every((player) =\u003e player.hand.length === 0)) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 882,
            "text": "      this.scoreHand();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 883,
            "text": "      this.game.handDone = true;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 884,
            "text": "      this.game.phase = \"done\";"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 885,
            "text": "      this.game.waitingForHuman = false;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 886,
            "text": "    } else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 887,
            "text": "      this.game.phase = \"play\";"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 888,
            "text": "      this.updateHumanWait();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 889,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 890,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 891
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 892,
            "text": "  collectKlopGift(winner) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 893,
            "text": "    if (this.game.contract.id !== \"klop\") return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 900,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1111,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Tarok Table"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "A self-contained browser prototype of Slovenian-style Tarok for 3 or 4 players."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "Serve this folder with any static server and open the local URL. The app uses ES modules, so &#96;file://&#96; double-clicking is not the target path."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "python3 -m http.server 8787 -d tarok-browser"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "Then open &#96;http://127.0.0.1:8787&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "## Player Model"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "- Seat 0 is the human south seat: \"You\" / \"Vi\"."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "- Opponents are local AI seats."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "- AI levels: Easy, Medium, Hard."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "- Human bidding and card play are interactive; talon exchange and discards are currently auto-assisted."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "- Contract bidding is now turn-based and interactive for the human seat."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "- Card deal/play events use DOM animation hooks so cards move across the table instead of snapping instantly."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "- English and Slovenian UI text are available from the language selector."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "## Card Assets"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "- Card faces are PNG assets in &#96;assets/cards/faces&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "- Card backs are in &#96;assets/cards/back.png&#96;."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "- &#96;assets/cards/generated-template-sheet.png&#96; is the image-generator source sheet."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "- Rebuild the full deck with:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "python3 tarok-browser/scripts/build-card-assets.py"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "## Current Rules Model"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "Rules are tracked against Briskula.si's Tarok rules at https://briskula.si/tarok."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "- 54-card Tarok deck: 22 taroks and four 8-card suits."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "- Four-player and three-player deals with a six-card talon."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "- Anti-clockwise trick play."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "- Follow suit if possible; otherwise play a tarok if possible."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "- Emperor trick: if Tarok I, Mond, and Škis appear in one trick, Tarok I wins."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "- Traditional three-card point counting with 70 total card points."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "- Positive contracts need at least 36 card points."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "- Turn-based bidding covers klop, three/two/one, piccolo, solo three/two/one, beggar, solo without, open beggar, colour valat without, and valat without."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "- Four-player normal contracts call a king for a hidden partner; three-player contracts are solo."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "- Talon exchange and discards are automated."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "- Point system includes contract value, point difference, unannounced trula/kings/pagat ultimo/king ultimo, valat, captured Mond, radlci, and klop scoring."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "- Klop uses talon gifts on the first six tricks."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 49
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3963063955307007
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 110,
            "text": "export function wouldWin(card, trick, contract = null) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "export function countTarokPoints(cards) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "  const total = cards.reduce((sum, card) =\u003e sum + card.value, 0);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118,
            "text": "  const groups = Math.floor(cards.length / 3);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "  const remainder = cards.length % 3;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120,
            "text": "  return total - groups * 2 - (remainder ? 1 : 0);"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "/** Human-facing tarok name: never \"XXII\" — Mond is XXI, Škis is not numbered. */"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 84,
            "line": 122
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 109
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/rules.js",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3952103853225708
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "export class TarokGame {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 734
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 735
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 736,
            "text": "  exchangeTalon() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 737,
            "text": "    const count = this.game.contract.talonTake;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 738,
            "text": "    if (!count) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 739,
            "text": "      const talonCount = this.game.talon.length;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 740,
            "text": "      this.game.talonTaken = [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 741,
            "text": "      if (this.game.contract.id === \"klop\") {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 742,
            "text": "        this.log(\"talonNoExchange\", { declarerId: this.game.declarer, count: talonCount });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 743,
            "text": "        return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 744,
            "text": "      }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 745,
            "text": "      this.game.talonRejected = [...this.game.talon];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 746,
            "text": "      this.game.talon = [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 747,
            "text": "      this.log(\"talonNoExchange\", { declarerId: this.game.declarer, count: talonCount });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 748,
            "text": "      return;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 749,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 750
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 751,
            "text": "    const declarer = this.players[this.game.declarer];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 752,
            "text": "    const groups = this.talonGroups(count);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 753,
            "text": "    const choice = this.controllers[declarer.id].chooseTalonGroup(this.game, declarer, groups);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 754,
            "text": "    const taken = groups[choice] || groups[0];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 755,
            "text": "    const rejected = groups.flatMap((group, index) =\u003e (index === choice ? [] : group));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 756,
            "text": "    declarer.hand.push(...taken);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 757,
            "text": "    sortHand(declarer.hand);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 758
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 759,
            "text": "    const discards = this.controllers[declarer.id]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 760,
            "text": "      .chooseDiscard(this.game, declarer, count)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 761,
            "text": "      .filter((card) =\u003e canDiscard(card))"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 762,
            "text": "      .slice(0, count);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 763,
            "text": "    this.finalizeTalonExchange(taken, rejected, discards);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 764,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 765
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 766,
            "text": "  talonGroups(count) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 767,
            "text": "    const groups = [];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 772,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1111,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "export class TarokGame {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 419
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 420
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 421,
            "text": "  placeBid(playerId, contract) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 422,
            "text": "    if (this.game.phase !== \"bidding\" || this.game.activePlayer !== playerId) return false;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 423,
            "text": "    const bidding = this.game.bidding;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 424,
            "text": "    if (!contract \u0026\u0026 bidding.forehandChoice) contract = CONTRACTS.klop;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 425,
            "text": "    if (contract) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 426,
            "text": "      const adapted = this.adaptContractForPlayers(contract);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 427,
            "text": "      bidding.currentContract = adapted;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 428,
            "text": "      bidding.highestBidder = playerId;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 429,
            "text": "      bidding.passesSinceRaise = 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 430,
            "text": "      this.players[playerId].bid = adapted;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 431,
            "text": "      bidding.history.push({ playerId, contractId: adapted.id, round: bidding.round });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 432,
            "text": "      this.log(\"bidContract\", { playerId, contractId: adapted.id, round: bidding.round });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 433,
            "text": "    } else {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 434,
            "text": "      if (!bidding.passedPlayers.includes(playerId)) bidding.passedPlayers.push(playerId);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 435,
            "text": "      bidding.passesSinceRaise += 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 436,
            "text": "      bidding.history.push({ playerId, contractId: null, round: bidding.round });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 437,
            "text": "      this.log(\"bidPass\", { playerId, round: bidding.round });"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 438,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 439
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 440,
            "text": "    bidding.totalActions += 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 441,
            "text": "    if (this.isBiddingDone()) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 442,
            "text": "      this.finishBidding();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 443,
            "text": "      return true;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 444,
            "text": "    }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 445,
            "text": "    this.advanceBidder();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 446,
            "text": "    this.updateHumanWait();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 447,
            "text": "    return true;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 448,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 449
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 450,
            "text": "  isBiddingDone() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 451,
            "text": "    const bidding = this.game.bidding;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 468,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1111,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],
        "range": {

            "column": 1,

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "export class TarokGame {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "  startSession({ playerCount = this.playerCount, aiLevel = this.aiLevel } = {}) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "    this.playerCount = Number(playerCount);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "    this.aiLevel = aiLevel;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "    this.players = PLAYER_NAME_KEYS.map((nameKey, index) =\u003e ({"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "      id: index,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "      nameKey,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "      active: index \u003c this.playerCount,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "      hand: [],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "      taken: [],"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "      tricks: 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "      bid: null,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "      radli: 0,"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "      human: index === this.humanId"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "    }));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "    this.controllers = this.players.map((player) =\u003e ("
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "      player.human ? new HumanController(player.id) : new LocalAIController(player.id, this.aiLevel)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "    ));"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "    this.dealer = this.playerCount - 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "    this.scores = [0, 0, 0, 0];"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "    this.handNumber = 0;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "    this.startHand();"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "  startHand() {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "    this.handNumber += 1;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112,
            "text": "  }"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 1111,
            "text": "}"
          }
        ],

    {
      "codeBlock": {

          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "export class TarokGame {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 505
          },

          },

            "text": "function evaluateHand(hand) {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "  const taroks = hand.filter(isTarok);"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "  const highTaroks = taroks.filter((card) =\u003e card.tarok \u003e= 17).length;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "  const trula = hand.filter(isTrula).length;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "  const kings = hand.filter((card) =\u003e card.rank === \"K\").length;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "  const counters = hand.filter((card) =\u003e card.value \u003e= 4).length;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "  const losers = hand.filter((card) =\u003e !isTarok(card) \u0026\u0026 card.value === 1).length;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "  const score = taroks.length * 3 + highTaroks * 4 + trula * 6 + kings * 3 - losers;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155,
            "text": "  return { score, taroks: taroks.length, highTaroks, counters };"
          },

            "text": "}"

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
For AI calling, `processCalling` invokes `this.` AI always calls `callKing()` without passing a `selectedKing`, defaulting to `autoCalledKing()`. The user wants heuristic-based king selection, so we should use `controllers[declarer].chooseCalledKing(game, player)` or integrate this logic into `game.js`.

The current `processCalling()` method calls `callKing()` directly and then proceeds to the next phase. Since `callKing()` uses `selectedKing || this.autoCalledKing()`, we need to either:

- Use `controllers[declarer].chooseCalledKing(game, player)` for heuristic-based selection
- Integrate the king selection logic into `game.js` - Change `processCalling` to get the king from the controller when the player is not human
- Improve `autoCalledKing` to use the same heuristic module
- Add `chooseCalledKing(game, player)` to `LocalAIController`
- Have `callKing` accept the selected king from the game when called by AI
- For human players, `chooseHumanCalledKing` already passes the king
- For AI players, `processCalling` should call `chooseCalledKing` from the controller instead of relying on `autoCalledKing`</details></think>

{
  "name": "Tarock AI strategies",
  "overview": "Implement a layered AI system centered on a shared hand feature evaluator, contract-aware bidding and card play, talon scoring, optional partner inference, and three difficulty tiers—building on [src/ai.js](src/ai.js), [src/game.js](src/game.js), and [src/rules.js](src/rules.js).",
  "plan": "# Tarock AI strategies (per contract type)\n\n## Current codebase anchors\n\n- **Contracts and modes** are defined in [&#96;src/rules.js&#96;](src/rules.js) (&#96;CONTRACTS&#96;, &#96;legalCards&#96;, &#96;wouldWin&#96;, &#96;trickWinner&#96;, &#96;negativeLegalSubset&#96; for klop/beggar/open beggar/piccolo Pagat rules, &#96;colourValat&#96; / &#96;valat&#96; modes).\n- **Game loop and AI hooks**: [&#96;src/game.js&#96;](src/game.js) calls &#96;chooseBid(this.game, player)&#96;, &#96;chooseTalonGroup(this.game, player, groups)&#96;, &#96;chooseDiscard(...)&#96;, &#96;chooseCard(this, legal, player)&#96; (**note:** bid/talon use the inner &#96;game&#96; object; play receives the full &#96;TarokGame&#96; instance—new code should treat this explicitly, e.g. always accept &#96;tarokGame&#96; + document which fields are read).\n- **Calling a king**: AI currently uses only [&#96;autoCalledKing()&#96;](src/game.js) (&#96;processCalling&#96; → &#96;callKing()&#96; with no args). Humans use &#96;chooseHumanCalledKing&#96;. The plan adds a controller hook so AI applies your king-selection heuristic instead of the simplistic sort.\n- **Gap**: &#96;openBeggar.openHand&#96; exists on the contract object but is **not** used in [&#96;src/game.js&#96;](src/game.js) to expose the declarer’s hand after trick 1. Full minimax for open beggar (as in your spec) assumes that visibility; either add a small engine state (&#96;game.openBeggarHandRevealed&#96; + optional &#96;visibleHands&#96; for AI) or scope v1 to heuristics until that lands.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\nIntroduce a small &#96;src/ai/&#96; package (keep [&#96;src/ai.js&#96;](src/ai.js) as a thin facade exporting &#96;LocalAIController&#96; and wiring):\n\n| Module | Responsibility |\n|--------|----------------|\n| &#96;handEvaluator.js&#96; | Build &#96;features&#96; + &#96;positiveStrength&#96;, &#96;negativeStrength&#96;, &#96;soloStrength&#96;, &#96;klopRisk&#96; (map Škis/Mond/Pagat, tarok bands to your spec—align numeric bands with existing helpers like &#96;cardPower&#96; / &#96;tarok&#96; where useful). |\n| &#96;bid.js&#96; | For each **legal** contract (same filter as [&#96;legalBidContracts()&#96;](src/game.js)), compute EV-style score; compare to &#96;passEV + riskMargin&#96; and forehand klop special case. |\n| &#96;kingCall.js&#96; | Score callable kings using your formula; handle edge case “all kings in hand” (legal self-call is strategically weak—prefer lower-scoring solo path or least-bad king per heuristic). |\n| &#96;talon.js&#96; | For each talon group: simulate &#96;hand + group&#96; → [&#96;findBestLegalDiscard&#96;](can reuse &#96;canDiscard&#96; from rules) → aggregate score (win proxy + banked discard points + void/structure bonuses − exposed-trump penalty). |\n| &#96;play/&#96; | One module per family: &#96;klop.js&#96;, &#96;negative.js&#96; (beggar + open beggar + piccolo branches), &#96;positive.js&#96;, &#96;valat.js&#96;, &#96;colourValat.js&#96;, &#96;piccolo.js&#96; (exactly-one-trick goal differs from beggar). |\n| &#96;announcements.js&#96; | Bonus / kontra decisions using separate EV from main line (respect [&#96;canAnnounceBonus&#96;](src/game.js), &#96;noBonuses&#96;, &#96;kingUltimo&#96; requires &#96;calledKing&#96; and not only talon). |\n| &#96;inference.js&#96; (optional v2) | Update per-opponent card sets from revokes, void inferences, high-trump signals; feed into Hard play and king-risk terms. |\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  hand[hand cards] --\u003e feat[handEvaluator]\n  feat --\u003e bid[bid.js]\n  feat --\u003e talon[talon.js]\n  feat --\u003e play[play router]\n  state[trick bidding scores] --\u003e bid\n  state --\u003e play\n  inference[inference.js] --\u003e play\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Play routing**: &#96;chooseCard&#96; delegates on &#96;game.game.contract.id&#96; and &#96;game.game.contract.mode&#96;, and role from &#96;tarokGame.isDeclarerSide&#96; / partner logic—not a single monolith.\n\n---\n\n## 1) Hand evaluator (shared foundation)\n\nImplement the feature struct you listed, computed from a &#96;Card[]&#96; hand plus optional context (e.g. contract class for “talonDependency” proxy before talon: use short-suit counts + high unprotected cards).\n\n- Reuse [&#96;isTarok&#96;](src/rules.js), [&#96;isTrula&#96;](src/rules.js), &#96;SUITS&#96; for per-suit lengths and high-card counts (&#96;rank&#96; K/Q/N/J… + &#96;suitStrength&#96;).\n- **Estimates** (&#96;sureWinnersEstimate&#96;, &#96;unavoidableWinnerEstimate&#96;, &#96;mondCaptureRisk&#96;, &#96;pagatUltimoPotential&#96;, etc.): start with deterministic heuristics (e.g. winners where no remaining card in suit can beat—requires a trivial “remaining deck” model from &#96;createDeck()&#96; minus known cards when full info only; for partial info use upper bounds). Document that full accuracy waits on &#96;inference.js&#96;.\n- Export composite scores with your baseline weights; keep weights in one config object for future self-play tuning.\n\n---\n\n## 2) Bidding strategy\n\n- Input: &#96;player.hand&#96;, [&#96;game.bidding&#96;](src/game.js) (current contract / rank), &#96;game.playerCount&#96;, &#96;legalBidContracts()&#96;-equivalent list (prefer passing **contract ids** from engine or duplicating filter in one place to avoid drift).\n- **Easy**: contract-specific **threshold tables** derived from your spec (klop: never bid; three/two/one/solos/beggar/valat…) + single scalar score from &#96;positiveStrength&#96; / &#96;negativeStrength&#96; when the table allows a candidate.\n- **Medium**: same as easy but use full &#96;features&#96; + weighted EV-like sum (win/lose/bonus/risk placeholders).\n- **Hard**: optional first step—**lightweight Monte Carlo**: sample random opponent hands consistent with nothing (v0), estimate &#96;P(win)&#96; for positive contracts from deal outcomes; keep your thresholds as fallback when sample budget is low. Defer full **MCTS / self-play** as a later milestone.\n\nMap game names to code ids:\n\n| Spec | &#96;CONTRACTS&#96; id |\n|------|----------------|\n| three / tri | &#96;three&#96; |\n| two / dva | &#96;two&#96; |\n| one / ena | &#96;one&#96; |\n| solo three… | &#96;soloThree&#96;, &#96;soloTwo&#96;, &#96;soloOne&#96; |\n| berač | &#96;beggar&#96; |\n| solo brez | &#96;soloWithout&#96; |\n| odprti berač | &#96;openBeggar&#96; |\n| barvni valat brez | &#96;colourValatWithout&#96; |\n| valat brez | &#96;valatWithout&#96; |\n| piccolo (exactly one trick) | &#96;piccolo&#96; (add dedicated bidding branch; not “berač”) |\n\n**3-player**: [&#96;adaptContractForPlayers&#96;](src/game.js) already drops &#96;callsKing&#96; and forces solo-style contracts—bidding module should branch on &#96;playerCount === 3&#96; (no partner EV, higher solo emphasis).\n\n---\n\n## 3) Called king selection\n\n- Add &#96;chooseCalledKing(tarokGame, player)&#96; on [&#96;LocalAIController&#96;](src/ai.js) (and default on &#96;SeatController&#96; stub).\n- In [&#96;processCalling&#96;](src/game.js) (or a dedicated &#96;processAiCalling&#96;): if declarer is AI, resolve king from controller **before** &#96;callKing(king)&#96;; keep &#96;autoCalledKing()&#96; as fallback.\n- Implement scoring over [&#96;callableKings()&#96;](src/game.js): short suit bonus, ability to trump later (proxy: tarok count + void potential), danger of capture (Mond/Skis awareness), king-in-talon forcing solo—use &#96;game.talon&#96; length only as “unknown” (6 cards), not exact contents unless you deliberately cheat for AI omniscience (default: **no** omniscience for fairness).\n\n---\n\n## 4) Talon strategy\n\n- For each group from [&#96;talonGroups&#96;](src/game.js): merge into hand, run **discard search**: choose &#96;count&#96; discardable cards (&#96;canDiscard&#96;) maximizing your scoring function (points banked + structural void + bonus potential − exposing critical trumps). Brute force is feasible for small counts (e.g. C(n,k) for typical n≈15, k=1..3).\n- **Easy**: max sum &#96;card.value&#96; + small tie-break (current behavior, slightly smarter).\n- **Medium/Hard**: full discard optimizer + optional rollout (Hard) sampling random completions of talon rejected cards for defenders.\n\n---\n\n## 5) Strategies per game (play phase)\n\nCentral **&#96;choosePlay(tarokGame, player, legalCards)&#96;** that switches on contract:\n\n| Contract / mode | Play approach (v1 → v2) |\n|-----------------|---------------------------|\n| **klop** | Implement your priority list using &#96;wouldWin&#96;, &#96;legalCards&#96;; treat first **6** tricks specially via &#96;game.trickNumber&#96; vs klop gift rule in [&#96;collectKlopGift&#96;](src/game.js). |\n| **beggar / openBeggar** | Forced-win avoidance using &#96;negativeLegalSubset&#96;; beggar declarer: your min-risk scorer. Open beggar: **heuristic v1** until open-hand state exists; then minimax/depth-limited search for Hard. |\n| **piccolo** | Declarer wants **exactly one** trick—different from beggar; branch separate from &#96;isNegativeContract&#96; routing. |\n| **positive / normal** | Universal scorer: immediate trick value + control + bonus progress + Mond/Pagat risk; partner/defender branches (&#96;isDeclarerSide&#96;, &#96;partnerKnownPublicly&#96;). |\n| **soloWithout** | Emphasize safe 36+ line; assume defenders conceptually richer from [&#96;rejectedTalonForDefenders&#96;](src/game.js) logic when scoring “talon to defenders.” |\n| **colourValatWithout** | Per your spec: rely on **simulation / guaranteed-win checks** using &#96;colourValat&#96; rules in [&#96;trickWinner&#96;](src/rules.js); lead only guaranteed winners when possible. |\n| **valatWithout** | Normal trump rules; push toward exhaustive or heavy partial search for Hard v2. |\n\n**Klop defensive** “don’t save opponent” is low priority v1—optional team signal heuristics later.\n\n---\n\n## 6) Bonuses (announcement phase)\n\nImplement [&#96;chooseAnnouncement&#96;](src/ai.js) for AI seats using [&#96;canAnnounceBonus&#96;](src/game.js) / [&#96;canDoubleGame&#96;](src/game.js):\n\n- Separate EV for **trula**, **kings**, **pagat ultimo**, **king ultimo**, **valat** announcement vs failure penalties from [&#96;scoreBonuses&#96;](src/game.js) / [&#96;scoreAnnouncedBonusAdjustments&#96;](src/game.js).\n- **Easy**: usually pass; occasional obvious trula/kings if hand has almost all pieces.\n- **Medium+**: EV gating + control estimates from hand evaluator.\n\n---\n\n## 7) Information model (Medium+ / Hard)\n\nMaintain per-opponent **possible holding sets** updated after each trick:\n\n- When a player **cannot follow suit**: remove that suit’s cards from their possibles (and handle colour-valat trump-follow nuances via existing legality).\n- When someone avoids trumping despite void: refine (rules.js already encodes some negative restrictions).\n- Feed **&#96;partnerProbability&#96;** from called king + observed plays for partner-aided lines in positive games.\n\nPersist this on &#96;TarokGame&#96; or a dedicated &#96;AiContext&#96; object attached only to AI controllers to avoid bloating saves/replays—design choice in implementation.\n\n---\n\n## 8) Three difficulty tiers (mapping to your spec)\n\n| Tier | Bidding | Talon | Play | Announcements | Inference |\n|------|---------|-------|------|---------------|-----------|\n| **easy** | Threshold tables; random jitter optional | Random or naive high-value group | Mostly “lowest winning / highest losing” within legals; klop/beggar special short-circuits | Pass | None |\n| **medium** | Heuristic EV + features | Feature scoring + optimal discard | Contract routers + universal scorer + partner heuristics | EV-lite | Basic void inference only |\n| **hard** | + optional MC bidding | + light rollouts | + minimax/depth search for beggar/open beggar/valat family when feasible; MC play for normal games | Full EV gating | Partner odds + stronger inference |\n\n[&#96;HumanController&#96;](src/ai.js) already delegates some tutor behavior to &#96;LocalAIController&#96; for bidding/talon—extend **only if** you want hints to match new logic (optional).\n\n---\n\n## Testing and validation\n\n- **Unit tests** (if you add a test runner) for: &#96;evaluateHand&#96; aggregates, &#96;legalBid&#96; filtering alignment, king scorer monotonicity smoke tests, &#96;chooseDiscard&#96; optimality on toy hands.\n- **Deterministic seeds**: reuse deck seeding if you add it; otherwise manual &#96;createDeck()&#96; + fixed shuffle for regression fixtures.\n- **Manual smoke**: run [&#96;autoplayTurnLimit&#96;](src/game.js) across &#96;aiLevel&#96; values and watch for illegal bids (must intersect &#96;legalBidContracts&#96;).\n\n---\n\n## Suggested implementation order\n\n1. &#96;handEvaluator&#96; + wire into existing &#96;evaluateHand&#96; in [&#96;src/ai.js&#96;](src/ai.js).\n2. Refactor bidding to use legal contract list + per-contract thresholds / EV.\n3. King call controller hook + [&#96;kingCall.js&#96;](src/ai/kingCall.js).\n4. Talon group + discard optimizer.\n5. Play router: klop + positive first (most hands), then beggar/piccolo, then valat variants.\n6. Announcements EV.\n7. Inference + Hard search passes.\n\nThis order maximizes visible improvement early while deferring the heaviest solver work to contracts where it matters most.\n",
